input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**
```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `ObservableCollection<Item> Items` | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` → `Item SelectedItem` | Tracks selection (set back to `null` after navigating) |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` → `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Triggers navigation to detail page |
| `RefreshCommand` → `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Calls your API to reload data |
| `IsRefreshing` → `bool IsRefreshing` | Controls the refresh spinner |

**ViewModel sketch (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**
```csharp
[ObservableProperty] ObservableCollection<Item> items = new();
[ObservableProperty] Item? selectedItem;
[ObservableProperty] bool isRefreshing;

[RelayCommand]
async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
{
    if (SelectedItem is null) return;
    await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"detail?id={SelectedItem.Id}");
    SelectedItem = null; // reset so user can re-select same item
}

[RelayCommand]
async Task RefreshAsync()
{
    var data = await _apiService.GetItemsAsync();
    Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
    IsRefreshing = false;
}
```

**Key points:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigation so the same item can be tapped again.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` when the refresh completes or the spinner won't dismiss.
- `SelectionMode="Single"` ensures only one item highlights at a time.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) CollectionView implementation with single item selection and pull-to-refresh functionality. They want XAML and ViewModel binding explanations. This is a general knowledge question that doesn't require any tools - I can answer directly.
